{{Short description|Protein-coding gene in humans}}
{{Infobox gene}}
'''Calcium homeostasis modulator 1''' (CALHM1) is a pore-forming subunit of a [[voltage-gated ion channel]] and a voltage-gated ATP channel that in humans is encoded by the ''CALHM1'' [[gene]].<ref name="pmid18585350">{{cite journal | vauthors = Dreses-Werringloer U, Lambert JC, Vingtdeux V, Zhao H, Vais H, Siebert A, Jain A, Koppel J, Rovelet-Lecrux A, Hannequin D, Pasquier F, Galimberti D, Scarpini E, Mann D, Lendon C, Campion D, Amouyel P, Davies P, Foskett JK, Campagne F, Marambaud P | title = A polymorphism in CALHM1 influences Ca2+ homeostasis, Abeta levels, and Alzheimer's disease risk | journal = Cell | volume = 133 | issue = 7 | pages = 1149–61 | date = Jun 2008 | pmid = 18585350 | doi = 10.1016/j.cell.2008.05.048 | pmc=2577842}}</ref><ref name="Taruno_2013">{{cite journal | vauthors = Taruno A, Matsumoto I, Ma Z, Marambaud P, Foskett JK | title = How do taste cells lacking synapses mediate neurotransmission? CALHM1, a voltage-gated ATP channel | journal = BioEssays: News and Reviews in Molecular, Cellular and Developmental Biology | volume = 35 | issue = 12 | pages = 1111–8 | date = December 2013 | pmid = 24105910 | pmc = 3981604 | doi = 10.1002/bies.201300077 }}</ref>

== Function ==

===Central nervous system ===

CALHM1 was identified by a tissue-specific gene expression profiling approach<ref name="pmid11691939">{{cite journal | vauthors = Skrabanek L, Campagne F | title = TissueInfo: high-throughput identification of tissue expression profiles and specificity | journal = Nucleic Acids Research | volume = 29 | issue = 21 | pages = E102-2 | date = Nov 2001 | pmid = 11691939 | doi=10.1093/nar/29.21.e102 | pmc=60201}}</ref> that screened for genes located on susceptibility loci for late-onset [[Alzheimer's disease]] (AD) and that are preferentially expressed in the hippocampus,<ref name="pmid18585350"/> a brain region affected early in AD. CALHM1 is a plasma membrane calcium-permeable [[ion channel]] regulated by voltage and extracellular calcium levels.<ref name="pmid22711817">{{cite journal | vauthors = Ma Z, Siebert AP, Cheung KH, Lee RJ, Johnson B, Cohen AS, Vingtdeux V, Marambaud P, Foskett JK | title = Calcium homeostasis modulator 1 (CALHM1) is the pore-forming subunit of an ion channel that mediates extracellular Ca2+ regulation of neuronal excitability | journal = Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America | volume = 109 | issue = 28 | pages = E1963-71 | date = Jul 2012 | pmid = 22711817 | doi = 10.1073/pnas.1204023109 | pmc=3396471| bibcode = 2012PNAS..109E1963M | doi-access = free }}</ref> The exact function of CALHM1 in the brain is not completely understood, but studies have shown that CALHM1 controls neuronal intracellular calcium homeostasis and signaling, as well as calcium-dependent neuronal excitability and memory in mouse models.<ref name="pmid22711817"/><ref name="pmid23345406">{{cite journal | vauthors = Dreses-Werringloer U, Vingtdeux V, Zhao H, Chandakkar P, Davies P, Marambaud P | title = CALHM1 controls the Ca<sup>2+</sup>-dependent MEK, ERK, RSK and MSK signaling cascade in neurons | journal = J Cell Sci | volume = 126 | pages = 1199–206 | date = Mar 2013 | issue = Pt 5 | pmid = 23345406 | doi = 10.1242/jcs.117135 | pmc=4481642}}</ref><ref name="pmid 27066908">{{cite journal | vauthors = Vingtdeux V, Chang EH, Frattini SA, Zhao H, Chandakkar P, Adrien L, Strohl JJ, Gibson EL, Ohmoto M, Matsumoto I, Huerta PT, Marambaud P | title = CALHM1 deficiency impairs cerebral neuron activity and memory flexibility in mice | journal = Sci Rep | volume = 6 | number = 24250 | date = Apr 2016 | pmid = 27066908 | doi = 10.1038/srep24250 | pmc=4828655 | article-number=24250| bibcode = 2016NatSR...624250V }}</ref> Recent data have also shown that CALHM1 might facilitate the [[proteolytic]] degradation of the cerebral [[amyloid beta]] peptide, a culprit in AD [[pathogenesis]].<ref name="pmid25999473">{{cite journal | vauthors = Vingtdeux V, Chandakkar P, Zhao H, Blanc L, Ruiz S, Marambaud P | title = CALHM1 ion channel elicits amyloid-β clearance by insulin-degrading enzyme in cell lines and in vivo in the mouse brain | journal = J Cell Sci | date = May 2015 | pmid = 25999473 | doi=10.1242/jcs.167270 | volume=128 | issue = 13 | pages=2330–8 | pmc=4524106}}</ref>

=== Peripheral taste system ===

CALHM1 is expressed in [[taste bud]] cells where it controls [[purinergic receptor]]-mediated taste transduction in the [[gustatory system]].<ref name="pmid23467090">{{cite journal | vauthors = Taruno A, Vingtdeux V, Ohmoto M, Ma Z, Dvoryanchikov G, Li A, Adrien L, Zhao H, Leung S, Abernethy M, Koppel J, Davies P, Civan MM, Chaudhari N, Matsumoto I, Hellekant G, Tordoff MG, Marambaud P, Foskett JK | title = CALHM1 ion channel mediates purinergic neurotransmission of sweet, bitter and umami tastes | journal = Nature | volume = 495 | issue = 7440 | pages = 223–6 | date = Mar 2013 | pmid = 23467090 | doi = 10.1038/nature11906 | pmc=3600154| bibcode = 2013Natur.495..223T }}</ref><ref name="pmid24385952">{{cite journal | vauthors = Kinnamon SC, Finger TE | title = A taste for ATP: neurotransmission in taste buds | journal = Front Cell Neurosci | volume = 7 | page = 264 | date = Dec 2013 | pmid = 24385952 | doi = 10.3389/fncel.2013.00264 | pmc=3866518| doi-access = free }}</ref>

{{refend}}
